fact_changes.current_value: On 20 September 1999, ABB Holdings Pte Ltd issued a second employment contract effective from 1 February 2000 to Sher Hock Guan Charles, stating that he would undertake to maintain and preserve secrecy in all office matters and not to divulge any information to outside members, and that private participation in other business activities, especially in the company’s line of business, was strictly not allowed.
fact_changes.recommended_value: On 20 September 1999, the first plaintiff issued a second employment contract effective from 1 February 2000 to Sher Hock Guan Charles, stating that he would undertake to maintain and preserve secrecy in all office matters and not to divulge any information to outside members, and that private participation in other business activities, especially in our line of business, was strictly not allowed.

fact_changes.current_value: As at 4 January 2000, the ABB Singapore Group used an Employee Handbook that contained policies, terms and conditions applicable to employees, including provisions on private participation in other business activities, transfer and deployment, protection of company assets and information, and avoidance of conflicts of interest.
fact_changes.recommended_value: The ABB Singapore Group used an Employee Handbook that contained policies, terms and conditions applicable to employees, including provisions on private participation in other business activities, transfer and deployment, protection of company assets and information, and avoidance of conflicts of interest.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 18 says that at all material times the ABB Singapore Group utilised an Employee Handbook and describes its provisions; the specific 'as at 4 January 2000' dating is tied to the version produced in evidence and is not necessary for a pre-dispute background row.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the unnecessary specific date to avoid litigation-stage versioning and keep only neutral background.

fact_changes.current_value: Whether, based on the pre-2003 employment contracts and Employee Handbook, the scope of contractual and equitable obligations of senior management personnel extended to restricting their participation in external business ventures and competitive activities involving medium voltage switchgear products.
fact_changes.recommended_value: Whether the defendant's employment contracts and handbook imposed restrictions on outside business participation, confidentiality, and conflicts of interest.
fact_changes.evidence: The current issue statement incorporates later dispute framing about medium voltage switchgear products and liability assessment; for a temporal-wall-safe background row, only the neutral contractual scope question should remain.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Trim the issue to neutral pre-dispute background and remove outcome-oriented specifics.

fact_changes.current_value: ABB Holdings Pte Ltd issued and administered employment contracts and an Employee Handbook that set out restrictions on outside business participation, confidentiality, and conflicts of interest for senior personnel such as Sher Hock Guan Charles, and these documents formed the framework within which his subsequent conduct in relation to other companies and medium voltage product ventures would be assessed.
fact_changes.recommended_value: ABB Holdings Pte Ltd issued employment contracts and an Employee Handbook that set out restrictions on outside business participation, confidentiality, and conflicts of interest for senior personnel.
fact_changes.evidence: The phrase 'subsequent conduct in relation to other companies and medium voltage product ventures would be assessed' reflects litigation-stage framing and should not be included in a temporal-wall-safe background summary.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the evaluative and dispute-outcome framing.

fact_changes.current_value: The judgment analyses whether the Employee Handbook was contractually incorporated into successive employment contracts, interprets the scope of any obligation to promote the interests of the ABB Group, and determines which provisions are apt to be contractual terms. It concludes that certain Handbook clauses on conflicts of interest and protection of assets and information applied throughout the defendant’s employment, but that an obligation to promote the interests of the global ABB Group did not continue beyond the first contract. The court then uses these findings to frame the assessment of alleged breaches and liability.
fact_changes.evidence: This is pure court reasoning and judicial findings, which are prohibited by the Temporal Wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the judicial reasoning log entirely.

facts_to_remove.recommended_change: Remove evaluative and outcome-linked wording; keep only the neutral fact that the company manufactured miniature circuit breakers.

facts_to_remove.current_value: Whether the pre-2003 conduct of Sher Hock Guan Charles in relation to external companies and medium voltage product development created a conflict with his roles as President and director of ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd, given that this company manufactured low voltage products.

facts_to_remove.recommended_change: Remove the issue statement; it is not a pre-dispute fact.

facts_to_remove.current_value: Company officers and senior managers are avoid situations where their external business activities intersect with or create potential competition or conflicts with the product lines and commercial interests of the company for which they serve, as defined by their contractual terms and any incorporated policies.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is a legal rule and court-derived generalisation, not a factual background item available before the dispute outcome.

case_name: ABB Holdings Pte Ltd and Others v Sher Hock Guan Charles

audit_suggestion.case_name: ABB Holdings Pte Ltd and Others v Sher Hock Guan Charles

label_change.evidence: The row is for a plaintiff. The judgment states: 'There will be judgment for the second and third plaintiffs against the defendant...' and 'the claim of the first plaintiff against the defendant is dismissed.' ABB Industry Pte Ltd is one of the plaintiffs and the judgment is in its favour.
label_change.recommended_change: Change the substantive outcome label for this plaintiff row from a defendant-style label to the plaintiff outcome 'Claim Allowed'.

fact_changes.evidence: The source text states only that ABB Industry Pte Ltd 'carries on business as, inter alia, a manufacturer of switchgears and an installer of electrical works.' It does not give a date for this fact.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the invented date; keep the fact as undated background.

fact_changes.evidence: The source text says the first plaintiff 'is owned by ABB Asea Brown Boveri Ltd, a Swiss company' and is the sole shareholder of the second and third plaintiffs. It does not state that ABB Industry Pte Ltd is a wholly owned subsidiary of ABB Holdings Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Replace the incorrect corporate relationship with the source-supported ownership description, or remove if not needed for this row.

fact_changes.current_value: From 1 January 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of General Manager of ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.recommended_value: From January 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles was General Manager of the third plaintiff, ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 2 states: 'January 2001 to February 2003 – General Manager, third plaintiff.'
fact_changes.recommended_change: Correct the role description to match the source and avoid adding an exact start date not given in the heading.

fact_changes.current_value: From September 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles held the position of Vice-President and Head of Business Area Low Voltage Products, Automation Technology Products Division Asia South, in ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.recommended_value: From September 2001 to February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles was Vice-President and Head of Business Area Low Voltage Products, Automation Technology Products Division Asia South, third plaintiff.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 2 states: 'September 2001 to February 2003 – Vice-President and Head of Business Area Low Voltage Products, Automation Technology Products Division Asia South, third plaintiff.'
fact_changes.recommended_change: Align the wording with the source and remove the inserted 'in ABB Industry Pte Ltd' phrasing.

fact_changes.current_value: With effect from 1 January 2001, ABB Industry Pte Ltd became the employing entity for Sher Hock Guan Charles following a reorganisation of the ABB Singapore Group.
fact_changes.recommended_value: With retrospective effect to 1 January 2001, his employment was transferred from the second plaintiff to the third plaintiff.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 2 states: 'On 15 January 2001, pursuant to a new organisation structure ... his employment was transferred from the second plaintiff to the third plaintiff with retrospective effect to 1 January 2001.'
fact_changes.recommended_change: Correct the date and wording to match the source exactly.

fact_changes.current_value: In February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles left the employment of ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.recommended_value: After leaving the third plaintiff, the defendant joined Huadian as its General Manager; this appointment took effect on 3 March 2003.
fact_changes.evidence: The source says he left the third plaintiff and that his Huadian appointment took effect on 3 March 2003. It does not state that he left in February 2003 in this fact.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove or rewrite to avoid an unsupported exact departure date.

fact_changes.current_value: In late October 2002, while still employed by ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles attended the 10th anniversary celebrations of ABB Xiamen Switchgear Co Ltd in Xiamen, China.
fact_changes.evidence: This is pre-dispute background and is supported by the source, but the row is a plaintiff row and the surrounding narrative includes later litigation-stage material elsewhere. This fact itself is usable and not leakage.
fact_changes.recommended_change: No removal needed.

fact_changes.current_value: During the October 2002 ABB Xiamen anniversary event, representatives of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute informed Sher Hock Guan Charles that they were looking for a technical adviser for research and development projects relating to a new generation of medium voltage switchgear, circuit breakers and vacuum interrupters.
fact_changes.recommended_value: During conversations with Li Zhao Lin and Pei Zhengjiang at the October 2002 ABB Xiamen anniversary event, the defendant learnt that XIHARI was looking for a technical adviser for its research and development projects.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 52 states that during the event he learnt XIHARI was looking for a technical adviser; the detailed description in the row is more specific than the source.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Narrow the fact to the source-supported wording.

fact_changes.current_value: In November 2002, officers of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute discussed by telephone with Sher Hock Guan Charles their wish to engage an internationally known technical adviser and mentioned the name of Guenther Leonhardt.
fact_changes.recommended_value: In November 2002, Mr Pei and another officer of XIHARI, Zheng Jun, mentioned in telephone conversations that they wished to engage an internationally known technical adviser and mentioned Mr Leonhardt's name.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 52 states that Mr Pei and Zheng Jun made the telephone conversations and mentioned Mr Leonhardt.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Correct the names and wording to match the source.

fact_changes.current_value: Between 3 December 2002 and 3 February 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles and Guenther Leonhardt exchanged nine e-mails concerning enquiries from Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute about engaging Leonhardt as a technical adviser for research and development projects on medium voltage switchgear, circuit breakers and vacuum interrupters and as a technical controller for a manufacturing company that would produce and market such products in China and other Asian markets.
fact_changes.recommended_value: Between 3 December 2002 and 3 February 2003, nine e-mails were exchanged between the defendant and Mr Leonhardt concerning XIHARI's enquiries about engaging Mr Leonhardt as a technical adviser for R&D projects relating to the development of a new generation of medium voltage switchgear, circuit breakers and vacuum interrupters.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 52 states the nine e-mails and the subject matter; the added 'technical controller' and manufacturing-company detail comes from later narrative and is not stated in this sentence.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Limit the fact to the source-supported email exchange description.

fact_changes.current_value: In November 2002, an officer of Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute met Sher Hock Guan Charles in Singapore and asked him to check with Guenther Leonhardt on the possibility of engaging Leonhardt as a technical adviser.
fact_changes.recommended_value: In November 2002, Mr Pei of XIHARI met the defendant in Singapore and asked him to check with Mr Leonhardt on the possibility of engaging the latter as XIHARI's technical adviser.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 92 states this meeting and request.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Correct the source names and wording.

fact_changes.current_value: By the end of January 2003, Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd had substantially completed development and critical type testing of a vacuum circuit breaker product, with testing having started on 25 October 2002 and completed on 13 March 2003.
fact_changes.recommended_value: By 31 January 2003, Huadian had finished prototyping and the most critical type testing. The testing of the product had begun on 25 October 2002 and was fully completed on 13 March 2003.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 49 gives the source wording.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Use the exact source phrasing.

fact_changes.current_value: By the end of January 2003, Sher Hock Guan Charles had decided to leave the employment of ABB Industry Pte Ltd and had informed a senior officer, Tom Sjoekvist, of this decision.
fact_changes.recommended_value: By this time, he had already decided to leave the third plaintiff's employ and had told this to a senior officer, one Tom Sjoekvist.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 53 states this in relation to January 2003, but not 'by the end of January 2003' as an exact date.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the invented exact timing.

fact_changes.current_value: On 3 March 2003, after leaving ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles commenced employment as General Manager of Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd.
fact_changes.recommended_value: His employment as general manager commenced on 3 March 2003.
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 53 states: 'His employment as general manager commenced on 3 March 2003.'
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the unsupported 'after leaving ABB Industry Pte Ltd' wording.

fact_changes.current_value: By 28 March 2004, Gelpag mbH, a German company in which Sher Hock Guan Charles was a shareholder, had acquired 16.2% of the shares in Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd, alongside Fu Hsiang International Pte Ltd and Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute.
fact_changes.recommended_value: In late March 2004, Gelpag mbH purchased 16.2% of the shares in Huadian at par value and the defendant was appointed a director of Huadian. As at 20 March 2004, the shareholders of Huadian were Fu Hsiang (80.5%), XIHARI (3.3%) and Gelpag mbH (16.2%).
fact_changes.evidence: Paragraph 55 gives the source wording and date as 'late March 2004' and 'As at 20 March 2004'.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Correct the date and shareholder names to match the source.

fact_changes.current_value: On or about 5 or 6 March 2003, shortly after joining Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles telephoned Guenther Leonhardt and invited him to visit Huadian’s premises in Xiamen to discuss a potential technical consultancy role.
fact_changes.recommended_value: On 5 or 6 March 2003, he called Mr Leonhardt and invited him to visit Huadian's premises in Xiamen.

fact_changes.current_value: Whether, before and up to February 2003, the conduct of Sher Hock Guan Charles in assisting Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute to identify and engage a technical adviser, in facilitating the development and commercialisation of medium voltage switchgear products through Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd, and in participating in other business entities, conflicted with or was restricted by his contractual and senior management obligations to ABB Industry Pte Ltd.
fact_changes.evidence: This issue statement is framed around the dispute and includes later-developed Huadian commercialisation facts and litigation framing.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove or neutralise the issue statement for temporal-wall compliance.

fact_changes.current_value: Senior managers responsible for a business area are generally expected, under contract and general company law principles, to protect their employer’s commercial interests, avoid conflicts between their own external business activities and the employer’s operations, refrain from using their position to advance competing ventures, and disclose to their employer material information about external projects that may affect the employer’s markets or product lines.
fact_changes.evidence: This is legal characterization and reasoning, not neutral background fact.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove legal-rule narrative from the party row.

fact_changes.current_value: While serving as General Manager and Vice-President responsible for low voltage products at ABB Industry Pte Ltd, Sher Hock Guan Charles engaged with Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute about its plan to develop and market a new generation of medium voltage switchgear and circuit breakers in China and other Asian markets, introduced former ABB technical expert Guenther Leonhardt to that institute, and later joined and invested in Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd, which manufactured and marketed medium voltage products internationally. During the same period he also facilitated a property development project in Xiamen through Great Vision International Pte Ltd and was involved in the incorporation of Webmoney Investment (S) Pte Ltd. These pre-dispute actions created a factual context in which his external roles and the emerging medium voltage venture intersected with ABB Industry Pte Ltd’s activities in supplying switchgear products for projects, and with the contractual provisions on outside business participation and conflicts of interest.
fact_changes.evidence: This is a synthesis of facts plus argumentative framing and includes later events beyond the temporal wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the application narrative; keep only discrete pre-dispute facts if needed.

fact_changes.current_value: The court concludes that the defendant, as a very senior manager of ABB Industry Pte Ltd, owed fiduciary duties similar to those of a director, in addition to his contractual obligations under the employment contracts and incorporated Handbook. It finds that his assistance to Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute in securing the services of Guenther Leonhardt for the development and commercialisation of medium voltage products, which were intended to be marketed in China and other Asian markets and to compete at an international level, was inconsistent with his obligation to act in the best interests of ABB Industry Pte Ltd and to avoid conflicts of interest. The court reasons that he should have informed ABB Industry Pte Ltd of XIHARI’s plans and declined to assist in recruiting a technical adviser. It also analyses his involvement with Great Vision, Xiamen Fu Cheng, Webmoney, Gelpag International and Gelpag mbH, distinguishing between activities that were unrelated to ABB Industry Pte Ltd’s business and those that contributed to the establishment and success of Huadian as a competitor. On the totality of the evidence, the court holds that he was in breach of his contractual and fiduciary obligations to ABB Industry Pte Ltd and that liability on the issue of responsibility is established, with quantum to be determined separately.
fact_changes.evidence: This is judgment-stage reasoning and findings, which must not be used in the row under the Temporal Wall.
fact_changes.recommended_change: Remove the judicial reasoning log entirely.

facts_to_remove.current_value: An individual who holds senior management and board positions in a company is generally expected, under contract and general company law principles, to avoid external business activities that intersect with or compete against the company’s business, to refrain from using information or opportunities obtained through his position to advance separate ventures, and to disclose to his employer material information about external projects that may affect the employer’s commercial interests.

facts_to_remove.current_value: While serving as President, director, General Manager and Vice-President within ABB Singapore Group companies, Sher Hock Guan Charles engaged with Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute about its plan to develop and market medium voltage switchgear and circuit breakers, introduced former ABB technical expert Guenther Leonhardt to that institute, and later joined and invested in Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd, which manufactured and marketed medium voltage products internationally. During the same period he facilitated a property development project in Xiamen through Great Vision International Pte Ltd and Xiamen Fu Cheng Real Estate Property Development Co. Ltd, received a substantial financial return from that project which was used to fund an equity stake in Huadian via Gelpag mbH, and assisted in the incorporation and directorship of Webmoney Investment (S) Pte Ltd and Gelpag International Pte Ltd. These pre-dispute events created a pattern in which his external ventures and investments intersected with the product and geographic markets the ABB Singapore Group companies for which he held senior roles.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is a litigation-stage application and includes evaluative, outcome-linked language.
facts_to_remove.recommended_change: Remove the application narrative because it contains judgment-style synthesis and outcome-revealing characterization.

facts_to_remove.current_value: The court examines the defendant’s career path within the ABB Group, his contractual terms, and the Employee Handbook to determine the scope of his obligations. It concludes that he was a very senior employee who owed fiduciary duties to ABB Industry Pte Ltd and fiduciary duties as a director to ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd. The judgment finds that his assistance to Xian High Voltage Apparatus Research Institute in recruiting Guenther Leonhardt for the development and commercialisation of medium voltage products, and his subsequent role and investment in Xiamen Huadian Switchgear Co. Ltd, were inconsistent with his obligations to act in the best interests of the ABB companies and to avoid conflicts of interest. The court distinguishes between his involvement in the Xiamen property development project and Webmoney, which it treats as either not established as active participation during his ABB employment or as unrelated to ABB’s business, and his conduct relating to Huadian, which it treats as giving rise to liability. It ultimately holds that he is liable to ABB Industry Pte Ltd (and to a more limited extent to ABB Installation Materials (East Asia) Pte Ltd) for breaches of his contractual and fiduciary obligations, with the assessment of loss and quantum to be addressed in separate proceedings.
facts_to_remove.evidence: This is entirely judgment reasoning and outcome-stage material.
facts_to_remove.recommended_change: Remove the judicial reasoning log because it is post-dispute and outcome-revealing.
